moves.title: Concrete tactic candidate
moves.applicability: This tactic is already concrete for the current goal.
moves.effect: Committing this tactic would change the EasyCrypt proof state if EasyCrypt accepts it.
evidence: EasyCrypt reports no remaining goals after the latest tactic; commit `qed.` to save the lemma before final verification.

selected_handles.title: Concrete tactic candidate
selected_handles.why_relevant: EasyCrypt reports no remaining goals after the latest tactic; commit `qed.` to save the lemma before final verification.

notes.message: event log says candidate_closed and current EC state is indeterminate

steps.tactic: byphoare (_: i = i0 ==> res = a i0) => //.
while (0 <= j <= N /\ i = i0 /\ (j <= i0 => PIR.s = PIR.s') /\ (i0 < j => sxor2 PIR.s PIR.s' i0)) (N - j).
skip => &hr Hi0; split; first by smt().
move => s1 s' j0; split; first by smt().
move => hnlt [hb [hi2 [hle hsxi]]]; have h: sxor2 s1 s' i0 by apply hsxi; smt().
move: (big predT<:int> a u1) (big predT<:int> a u2) => A B.
have hz : A +^ B +^ A +^ B = zerow by rewrite -xorwA xorwK.
rewrite xorwC !big_cat big_consT; move: (big predT<:int> a u1) (big predT<:int> a u2) => A B; rewrite (xorwC (a i0) B) !xorwA.
have hz : A +^ B +^ A +^ B = zerow by rewrite -xorwA xorwK. by rewrite hz xorwC xorw0.

proof_so_far.note: Your committed proof so far in THIS node, step-numbered — your own work. Use it to orient; do not re-derive steps already listed here.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the lemmas that apply to an OPERATOR in your goal — project-local lemmas included (not just stdlib) — instead of guessing a lemma name.
ask_manager_for.returns: lemmas mentioning that operator, found by live EC `search` over the loaded context (statements; you pick which to apply)
